heading: Establishment of the Chief Executive Officer of the United States Agency for Global Media
label: 6203 Establishment of the Chief Executive Officer of the United States Agency for Global Media
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsecs. (b)(1) and (c), was in the original a reference to this Act, and was translated as referring to this title, meaning title III of , , , known as the United States International Broadcasting Act of 1994, which is classified principally to this chapter, to reflect the probable intent of Congress. Effective Date of 1998 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 105–277section 1301 of Pub. L. 105–277section 6531 of this titleOct. 1, 1999

References to Broadcasting Board of Governors, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 116–94, div. G, title I133 Stat. 2822Dec. 20, 2019“That any reference to the ‘Broadcasting Board of Governors’ or ‘BBG’, including in any account providing amounts to the Broadcasting Board of Governors, in any Act making appropriations for the Department of State, foreign operations, and related programs enacted before, on, or after the date of the enactment of this Act [] shall for this fiscal year, and any fiscal year thereafter, be construed to mean the ‘United States Agency for Global Media’ or ‘USAGM’, respectively.”Dec. 20, 2019

children.heading: Continued existence within Executive branch
children.label: a Continued existence within Executive branch
children.text: The United States Agency for Global Media shall continue to exist within the Executive branch of Government as an entity described in .section 104 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The head of the United States Agency for Global Media shall be a Chief Executive Officer, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. Notwithstanding any other provision of law, until such time as a Chief Executive Officer is appointed and has qualified, the current or acting Chief Executive Officer appointed by the Board may continue to serve and exercise the authorities and powers under this chapter.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 2 Term
children.text: The first Chief Executive Officer appointed pursuant to paragraph (1) shall serve for an initial term of three years.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 3 Compensation
children.text: A Chief Executive Officer appointed pursuant to paragraph (1) shall be compensated at the annual rate of basic pay for level III of the Executive Schedule under .section 5314 of title 5

children.heading: Termination of Director of International Broadcasting Bureau
children.label: c Termination of Director of International Broadcasting Bureau
children.text: Effective on , the position of the Director of the International Broadcasting Bureau shall be terminated, and all of the responsibilities, offices, authorities, and immunities of the Director or the Agency under this chapter or any other Act or authority before such date shall be transferred or available to, assumed by, or overseen by the Chief Executive Officer, as head of the Agency.December 23, 2016

children.heading: Immunity from civil liability
children.label: d Immunity from civil liability
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, all limitations on liability that apply to the Chief Executive Officer shall also apply to members of the boards of directors of RFE/RL, Inc., Radio Free Asia, the Open Technology Fund, the Middle East Broadcasting Networks, or any organization that consolidates such entities when such members are acting in their official capacities.
